metadata.title: Claude Connectors.

beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. A Connector is a one-click bridge between Claude and an app you pay for; over two hundred exist. Set up in four steps, then toggle an app on in any chat so Claude reads your real Gmail, Granola, or Slack. Nine useful ones span meetings, CRM, docs, backlogs, email, presentations, and calendar context. The rule is combine at least two: one prompt yields meeting summary, action items, and flagged blockers.

artifactLines: A Connector is a one-click bridge between Claude and an app you pay for; over two hundred exist.
Set up in four steps, then toggle an app on in any chat so Claude reads your real Gmail, Granola, or Slack.
Nine useful ones span meetings, CRM, docs, backlogs, email, presentations, and calendar context.
The rule is combine at least two: one prompt yields meeting summary, action items, and flagged blockers.

beats.narration_text: The rule to use at least two connectors combined is the part I haven't tried, and the weekly Granola prompt shows why. I want to build one combined workflow of my own. Help me (1) pair two of the nine connectors, say Granola and Linear, into a single prompt that turns a meeting into assigned backlog items, (2) adapt the reusable Granola template so it flags two to five blockers specific to engineering work, and (3) explain what context Claude actually holds across both connectors that a copy-paste workflow would lose.

props.command: The rule to use at least two connectors combined is the part I haven't tried, and the weekly Granola prompt shows why. I want to build one combined workflow of my own. Help me (1) pair two of the nine connectors, say Granola and Linear, into a single prompt that turns a meeting into assigned backlog items, (2) adapt the reusable Granola template so it flags two to five blockers specific to engineering work, and (3) explain what context Claude actually holds across both connectors that a copy-paste workflow would lose.

beats.narration_text: A Connector is a one-click bridge between Claude and an app you already pay for. Over two hundred of them. You probably use zero. The setup is four steps: open Claude, click the plus inside the chatbox, find Connectors, click Add Connector, pick your app. Then in any new chat, click plus again and toggle your app on. That's it.

shot.slotNote: SLOT — fill media/B01.png (screenshot: Claude chatbox with the + menu open, showing the Connectors option and the Add Connector button)

build.suggested: suggested prompt: A Connector is a one-click bridge between Claude and an app you already pay for. Over two hundred of them. You probably use zero. The setup is four steps: open

beats.narration_text: What changes when you turn a connector on? Everything. Gmail on: ask what's pending in your inbox this week — Claude reads your actual Gmail and answers. Zero copy-pastes. Granola on: ask for a summary of all last week's meeting transcripts — Claude reads them all. Zero dashboards opened. Slack on: ask Claude to extract an entire thread and list the action items — zero tabs visited. Claude has the full context of your Slack before it answers.

artifactLines: Gmail on → Claude reads your inbox. Zero copy-pastes.
Granola on → Claude reads all meeting transcripts. Zero dashboards.
Slack on → Claude pulls the full thread. Zero tabs.
Combine them: Granola + Gmail + Slack → one prompt, full context.

beats.narration_text: Nine useful connectors: Granola for meeting transcripts, HubSpot or Salesforce for lead follow-up, Notion for the team knowledge base, Linear for engineering backlogs, Google Drive for documents, Gmail for email drafts and triage, Slack for thread extraction, Gamma for presentations, and Google Calendar for scheduling context. The rule: use at least two at once, combined.

props.artifactHeading: The ones worth your fifteen minutes
artifactLines: Granola — meeting transcripts, action items, blockers.
HubSpot / Salesforce — deals stuck in pipeline, follow-up drafts.
Notion — team knowledge base queries without opening Notion.
Google Drive + Gmail + Slack — combine for full work context.

beats.narration_text: Here is a reusable weekly Granola prompt. Pull last meeting from Granola. Summarize in five bullets. List every action item assigned to me with deadlines. Flag two to five blockers that might prevent me completing those items. That is the wow moment of connectors — one prompt, full meeting context, action list, blockers. The meeting is already over. Claude already read it.

props.title: granola-weekly-prompt.md
props.code:
Pull my last meeting from Granola.
Summarize in 5 bullets.
Then list every action item assigned to me,
with deadlines.
Then flag the 2-5 blockers I might have
to complete some items.

beats.narration_text: And here's the HubSpot one — same pattern. Read the pipeline. List every deal in Proposal Sent that hasn't moved in seven-plus days. For each, write a follow-up email you can send today. Tone: direct, French-blunt-warm, no just-checking-in. You don't have to open HubSpot to read your HubSpot. And the pro tip: stop using HubSpot only as a CRM — use it as a research input.

props.title: hubspot-followup-prompt.md
props.code:
Read my HubSpot pipeline.
List every deal in "Proposal Sent"
that hasn't moved in 7+ days.
For each: write a follow-up email
I can send today.
Tone: direct, warm. No "just checking in."

beats.narration_text: Your turn. Book fifteen minutes. Pick three connectors. Connect them. Then paste this prompt to test all three at once.

props.command: Read my Gmail, my Granola meeting notes, and my Slack. Give me: (1) top 3 things I need to action today from email, (2) action items from my last meeting, (3) any open threads in Slack waiting on me. Prioritize by urgency.
props.runningText: paste this into Claude with connectors on…
